name: Lentils Ole
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup chopped scallions (green and white parts), or onion (either is good)
1 tablespoon chili powder (adjust to taste, especially if you use spicy chili powder)
1/2 teaspoon cumin seeds, crushed (what I use) or 1 teaspoon ground cumin
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the lentils and bring them and the water to a boil in a large pot.
2. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for about 25-30 minutes until soft but not mushy.
3. In a nonstick pan, sauté scallions, peppers, garlic, chili powder, and cumin seeds (or ground cumin) in the vinegar for about 2 minutes.
4. Add tomato sauce and molasses to the pan; cook for 1 minute, scraping any stuck pieces from the bottom of the pan.
5. Taste and add more vinegar if needed (I usually add another tablespoon).
6. Stir the mixture into the lentils and simmer until heated through.
7. Serve over rice or use as a burrito or taco filling (use less than 3 cups water for a less saucey result).
